title: Void Realm - An Otherworldly Solo Card Game
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNEGNjVWO0Q

Daniel (@dungeondive) checks out **Void Realm**, a solo card game from The Game Crafter that arrives in a VHS-style box—which he's become rather fond of lately.

You inhabit a vessel and travel through realms of unknowable beings who deal in life forces and ethereal energies. Collect five orbs before losing all your health, light, or accumulating too many burdens (guilt, shame, regret, despair, corruption). Three of the same burden? Journey over. Daniel reckons this might be the first "Doom Pilgrim-like" he's encountered—games where things happen *to* you rather than presenting neat strategic choices.

The art is wonderfully weird and esoteric. Perfect for candlelit October sessions if you're into that sort of thing.

----

*Anyone else drawn to games that prioritise atmosphere over agency? Share your forays into the deliberately strange.*
